dialog.text: Woof
Woof
Woof
Woof
Hah! You found me! Now, can you help me reach my friend?
Just kidding, I don't have a friend! Hah! I like to hide in remote places, but you don’t meet a lot of people doing that. Thanks for finding me!
Wanna be friends? I'll give you some stuff if you find me in weird locations.
Don't you think documenting animal species in your Codex is too easy? I have more ambition than that. I'm looking for sparked specimens! Unique, spark-infused creatures!
<objective>Two Sparkling creatures were spotted in the area: a slime and a bee. I need them both!</objective>
That's it! That's them! How'd you find those creatures? I have to reward you for your effort!
Now, I'll have to find a new track to follow. I'll let you know if I hear of another sparked specimen!
I'm sure you've got plenty of species documented in your Codex. But how many sparked specimens did you find?
I'm tracking 2 of them down right now! <objective>What I'm looking for is a Nepsid whale and a crab, both completely filled with spark</objective>.
That's it! That's them! How'd you find those creatures? I have to reward you for your effort!
Now, I'll have to find a new track to follow. I'll let you know if I hear of another sparked specimen!
Traveler, have you started filling up your codex, already? Look at this marvel! It keeps trace of every creature you've met, to advance the gradual recovery of human knowledge.

I was sent here by our chief encyclopedist to study the local fauna. You can help too, if you want. <objective>Show me a codex with at least five monsters in it</objective>, and I'll share my pay with you.
Now, let me see your codex… Man, nature really is a misunderstood artist… and you’ve done some first class scientific work! Thanks for your help.
We've struggled to rebuild human knowledge since our libraries burned 5 centuries ago. But now that we’re all back here together, I'm sure we'll make great progress!

The Crimson Rams used to come here everyday to kick the Nepsids out. But at one point, we figured it would teach those newcomers from Antelimbo a lesson if they had to do it themselves. Also, it’s cheaper for us.

Wanna prove to me you’re ready to enter the plains of Skover, lamb chop? Why don’t you <objective>go into the Nepsids’ hideout and kick Nepsilon’s butt</objective>? That’s their chief, Nepsilon. But you’ll find out real quick.
Did you really do it, lamb chop? Alright, you can have this. If I go back to the camp and tell them I clipped Nepsilon, I’ll probably get something better anyway.
Before the Chrysalis Age, Mayda was the biggest port in the whole region. Now… it’s still big and it's still a port, but we don’t control it anymore so what’s the point?
Queen sendzz mee… Wozz lookin’ for bbrecious fflower “Zzealotus”... Large fflower zhat growzz on pondzz…
Need zheeze for honeymakin’, ffiddle-bee-bee. <objective>Gazzer some bbrecious “Zzealotus” for mee.</objective>
Honey Moly, zhis izz zhe fflower! Givve mee… Zhen you can have zhee eligzzir.
Zhis izz gon’ bee vvery ffine honey, ffiddle-bee-bee.

Greetings, tenderfoot. I'm the old pioneer Otto Mata. Back in the Chrysalis Age, I was part of the few Pilgrims who survived in Siagarta. Spent my youth exploring the 6 regions of this world. Now, I'm trying to slow down.
I've been helping Hilda with her book by encouraging young adventurers to get involved. I'll reward anyone who can <objective>show me a codex filled with at least 20 entries</objective>.
Now, I've seen some curious creatures across this world, but I must say... this one looks like my great-great aunt. Well done, traveler, that's what we were looking for.
I'm just catching some rest. When the time comes, I'll be hitting the road again, just like you.
See these floating islands up there? I'm on a mission to explore the ruins that sit on top of them. Easy job, right? Well, it gets worse.
If I wanna face the golems again, I need to toughen up. I should <objective>find at least 5 motes of Kyre</objective>... Unfortunately, fighting golems is the best way to do that.
No way! Where did you find that? Don't tell me you just went up there and effortlessly collected golem husks...
Well, thank you! I was kinda hoping this would be my excuse not to do the job. Now, I guess I have no choice.
So, how does it feel to tread new ground for the first time? Siagarta is actually quite old... but it does feel new, doesn’t it?
I’m with the Pilgrims of Merilam. A faction of adventurers trying to map Siagarta and restore its ruins. They charged me with making sure you newcomers don’t get devoured by animals.

As you can see, this place is crawling with ferocious crabs. Why don't you <objective>show me you can defend yourself by bringing me one of their clamps</objective>? If you can do that, I’ll give you something useful.
Alright, I'd say you've got decent chances of survival! Now, here’s how you can further increase them: whenever you’re feeling lost, just look for the nearest Obelisk.
Obelisks are big stone pillars with little ponds around them. There's one right up that hill, you can't miss it. Now hit the road, Claude! Adventure awaits!
